runs.output: **Autonomous-loop authorization (the `mandate-008` exception for the deterministic loop, binding).** `mandate-008` — a subagent returns an issue → STOP, report, the user decides — governs when the continue/stop signal is an LLM judgement (a blocker, an ambiguity, a scope/value/authority call). It does NOT stop the iterate-to-zero loop of steps 2→3→4. Autonomous re-dispatch of a FRESH agent (a fix agent, then a fresh adversarial re-audit) IS permitted and REQUIRED iff the loop terminates on a DETERMINISTIC gate — `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` exit 0 and the runtime-demo real-check, never an LLM read of "looks clean" — AND a SEPARATE fresh adversarial audit (not the implementer) reports every acceptance criterion CONFIRMED. `done ≡ (deterministic gate exit 0) AND (every success criterion CONFIRMED)` — even if the audit's total findings are nonzero. **Audit posture:** the audit brief carries the task's filed `acceptance_criteria` verbatim; the auditor reports each criterion by id as CONFIRMED or a finding, never grades severity, never recommends; the orchestrator computes GO/NO-GO per criterion (any criterion false → NO-GO), and the per-criterion verdicts are filed as the verification item the turn the audit returns. Inside the loop the orchestrator ends each turn by dispatching the next agent (fix → re-audit → fix …) — its message terminates in the next tool call, never in a report that yields control — and surfaces to the user ONLY at a genuine LLM-judgement fork (a scope/value/authority decision, e.g. a HELD merge or release per the release-hold). A criterion-falsifying finding from the audit/probe is work the loop closes autonomously, scoped to the finding's whole class, not an issue handed to the user; handing a policy-determined criterion-falsifying fix to the user instead of looping it is the mandate-008 misread this exception forecloses. A finding that falsifies no criterion is FILED and not fixed in this task; trivia (cosmetic/style, language WARNs, pre-existing/unrelated/flaky) is non-blocking and recorded as a Flag.

runs.cmd: grep -rn 'Autonomous-loop' .context

history.note: new sweep finding, first grounding. The closure loop never ran; the substrate presents enacted parity as open next-work. Intent cross-check: IN-84 (gaps and issues sibling-class throughout), IN-108 (enact/fix, don't file), IN-129 (substrate is and remains current) support work-owed; no later intent contradicts. Broken-not-forgotten per the working-substrate gate: the stale statuses are re-observed on the live substrate.
